commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 19. "Wherefore hidest Thou Thy face:" as if Thou wert not present; as if thou hadst forgotten us? "And forgettest our misery and trouble?" (ver. 24).

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Dust. We are at death's door, being oppressed with grief, as long as thou dost not assist us. (Worthington)
We petition in the most fervent (Haydock) and humble posture, Isaias xlix. 23., and Micheas vii. 17. (Calmet)
